international_awards: Design of the Year 2013 (London Design Museum)

urban_context.zoning_constraints: PLU Paris heritage protection restrictions, Paris ring-road infrastructure acoustic buffers, strict perimeter footprint limits
urban_context.project_origin: Inversion and transformation of a post-war mass housing social tower targeted for demolition into a sustainable open habitat

tectonic_systems.structure: Original cast-in-place concrete post-beam frames reinforced with a lightweight self-supporting galvanized steel framework extensions matrix
tectonic_systems.facade: Unitized anodized aluminum sliding window wall with low-E multi-layered double glazing doors, integrated inside a 3-meter deep wintergarden balcony ring
tectonic_systems.environmental_tech: Passive thermodynamic wintergarden buffer zones, interior reflective thermal curtains, natural cross stack-effect ventilation layout, structural high-durability thermal break insulated pads

stage_2_knowledge_structure.site_context: Confronts the severe acoustic aggression and heavy particulate pollution of the adjacent Paris ring-road infrastructure. Inverts a socio-spatial residue into an ecological gateway.
stage_2_knowledge_structure.scale_and_geometry: Transforms a rigid modernist mineral solid into a porous crystalline envelope by extending floor plate projections 3 meters outward via a regular structural modular steel frame.
stage_2_knowledge_structure.user_sociology: Enforces profound socio-spatial dignity for social housing tenants by expanding living surfaces up to 50% without rent increases, creating a transitional buffer loggia that secures domestic safe territoriality.
stage_2_knowledge_structure.lifecycle_events: Pioneers an innovative dry prefabrication modular delivery timeline on an occupied construction site, saving over 60% of embodied carbon emissions compared to demolition-reconstruction models.
stage_2_knowledge_structure.performance_physics: The perimeter wintergarden acts as a passive thermodynamic heating/cooling engine reducing annual space-heating demands by over 50% while operating as an effective multicomponent sound trap.

stage_3_ontology_mapping.category: 4. 構造、材料與工程實務 (Tectonics, Materials & Construction)
stage_3_ontology_mapping.causal_dialectic: Prevents catastrophic structural thermal bridges where the new outdoor cantilevered lightweight steel framework elements couple mechanically with the historical cast-in-place concrete floor plates, avoiding internal wall condensation and heating losses in winter.

graphic_evidence_alignment.description: The technical joint layout blueprint details high-density insulation thermal separation pads sandwiched tightly at the steel anchor connection interface.

stage_3_ontology_mapping.category: 1. 生態與環境應對 (Ecology & Climate Adaptation)
stage_3_ontology_mapping.causal_dialectic: Optimizes solar heat gain control throughout varying seasonal path changes. The deep 3-meter cantilever horizontal floor plates cast direct geometric shadow profiles over lower glazed window layers during summer peak solar angles, eliminating artificial cooling requirements.

graphic_evidence_alignment.description: The technical building section marks out geometric sun angles proving how the overhanging upper balcony decks act as effective shade umbrellas for lower flat interiors.

stage_3_ontology_mapping.category: 4. 構造、材料與工程實務 (Tectonics, Materials & Construction)
stage_3_ontology_mapping.causal_dialectic: Solves structural weight limits and extreme high-altitude delivery hazards on an occupied site by shifting components to dry prefabrication modular logistics, gruting preassembled light concrete planks directly onto facade anchor brackets.

graphic_evidence_alignment.description: The floor grid charts a highly uniform 3m-span modular framework expanding symmetrically past the historical brick/concrete envelope line.

stage_3_ontology_mapping.category: 1. 生態與環境應對 (Ecology & Climate Adaptation)
stage_3_ontology_mapping.causal_dialectic: Combats urban heat island microclimate over-heating during summer cycles. The fully sliding double glazing facade windows unchain natural fluid cross-ventilation, drawing fresh breeze into the flat core driven by height-induced low-pressure zones.

graphic_evidence_alignment.description: The thermodynamic fluid section profile isolates natural wind vectors streaming through the deep open balcony cavities straight across the living rooms.

stage_3_ontology_mapping.category: 5. 空間行為學與公共利益 (Behavioral Realm & Public Benefit)
stage_3_ontology_mapping.causal_dialectic: Rejects the high-carbon capitalist tabula rasa demolition of modernist mass housing estates. Preserving the 1957 concrete skeleton while executing an active geometric slicing and facade extension enforces absolute social equity and low-carbon urban resource inversion.

graphic_evidence_alignment.description: High-resolution photographs explicitly document the contrast of the original modernist raw concrete shell wrapped seamlessly in the contemporary light open glass-and-steel bioclimatic skin.

stage_3_ontology_mapping.category: 6. 數位孿生與智能化運算 (Digital Twin & Smart Building)
stage_3_ontology_mapping.causal_dialectic: Absent by intent. The tower layout relies on human-driven low-tech adjustments (manual sliding windows and reflective interior thermal curtains) to prioritize tenant usage autonomy over complex, high-maintenance automated IoT kinetic machinery grids.

graphic_evidence_alignment.reason_of_gap: Design taxonomy preference of the late 2000s. Druot, Lacaton & Vassal prioritized low-maintenance material resilience, passive bioclimatic physics, and human domestic stewardship over computer-automated active building management systems, excluding motorized cabling telemetry graphics.
